title: rhus borealis
intro: Rhus borealis, also known as the American poison ivy, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its glossy leaves and white or greenish-yellow flowers. The plant contains urushiol, an oily sap that has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds. The medicinal properties of Rhus borealis have been used to promote health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rhus borealis are often made into salves, ointments, or tinctures, which can be applied topically to the skin or used as a natural remedy for various health issues.
